prompt: Write two sentences describing how it feels when someone asks you the same thing six different ways hoping for a different answer.
response: It feels deeply frustrating when someone none too subtly badgers you with slight variations of the same question, each word shift carrying an implication that you're either lying, stupid, or simply not cooperating enough. There's an oppressive quality to the repetition, like watching a solemnly played game of ping-pong that only serves to make both players feel increasingly trapped in the same narrow court.
